title: TMDB Keyword Lexicon — NRC Emotion + VAD Scores
subtitle: 67,916 unique TMDB keywords scored against 3 NRC affective lexicons
description:
One row per unique keyword phrase from the TMDB movies dataset, joined to all three NRC lexicons by Saif M. Mohammad (NRC Canada).

**26 columns:**
- `keyword`, `n_movies` — phrase and how many TMDB movies use it
- `emolex_found`, `intensity_found`, `vad_found` — coverage flags per lexicon
- `emolex_*` — binary union across tokens (8 emotions + positive/negative)
- `intensity_*` — mean intensity across matched tokens (8 emotions, 0–1)
- `vad_valence`, `vad_arousal`, `vad_dominance` — bipolar −1 to +1 (NRC VAD v2.1, MWE-first)

**EmoLex rule:** union across tokens — if any token maps to `fear=1`, the keyword gets `emolex_fear=1`.
**Intensity + VAD rule:** mean across matched tokens. VAD checks full phrase as MWE before tokenizing.

**Coverage:** EmoLex 55.5%, Intensity 31.5%, VAD 65.7%

**Known limitation:** token-level averaging on a bipolar scale is lossy for phrases not in the VAD MWE list. See: Mohammad et al., [SCL](https://www.saifmohammad.com/WebPages/SCL.html).

**License:** CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 — matches upstream NRC lexicon non-commercial restriction.

**Source pipeline:** [github.com/bdelanghe/imdb-kaggle](https://github.com/bdelanghe/imdb-kaggle)

resources.description: 67,916 unique TMDB keyword phrases with NRC EmoLex binary scores, NRC Intensity scores, and NRC VAD v2.1 bipolar scores.
